title: Automated scheduled refresh + diff review + re-license-gate
context: Knowledge graph data becomes stale as source databases update their records and occasionally change licenses. An automated refresh pipeline addresses the stale-evidence risk by re-running the ETL on updated source versions, re-applying the license gate, producing a diff for human review, and re-stamping as-of version labels. This is an unscheduled backlog item for after M4 is shipped.
objective: Implement an automated scheduled refresh pipeline that fetches updated source versions, re-applies the license gate before extraction, runs the ETL, produces a human-reviewable diff of changed assertions, and re-stamps as-of labels. A human must approve the diff before new data is published.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline runs on a configurable schedule and fetches the latest approved source versions.
License gate is re-applied at each refresh: if a source's terms have changed, the entry reverts to 'pending' and ingestion is blocked until re-approved.
A human-reviewable diff of added, removed, and changed assertions is generated for each refresh run.
As-of version labels are re-stamped on all assertions in the refreshed dataset.
Human approval is required before the refreshed data is published; no auto-publish without review.
Pipeline integrates with CI and passes all existing gates after refresh.
output: .github/workflows/refresh.yml and scripts/refresh/ directory implementing the scheduled refresh pipeline with diff generation, committed via PR.
